name: 있어요 & 없어요 — There Is / There Isn't

cards.grammarNote: 있어요 comes from 있다 (to exist / to have). Used for existence (there is) and possession (I have). 없어요 is the opposite.
없어요 = there is not / I don't have. 없다 is the negative of 있다. Always used as a single word — never with 안.

cards.front: It's here. / There is one here.
cards.grammarNote: 여기 있어요 = it's here. Also used to announce one's presence: 있어요 → I'm here!
Rising intonation turns 있어요 into a yes/no question. 버스 있어요? = Is there a bus? Is there a bus available?
없어요 directly follows the noun. No particle needed in casual spoken Korean for this construction.
어디 있어요? = where is (something)? Literally: where does it exist? 출구 = exit, 가 = subject marker.
근처에 = nearby (에 = location particle). 약국 = pharmacy. Add 에 to mark the location where something exists.
많이 (many/a lot) + 있어요 = there's a lot. 조금 있어요 = there's a little.
